title: monitoring — Eloquent observers
description: Six observers enforce the module's write-time invariants: health check config validation (interval floor, target/type match, PII scrub of response bodies, entitlement consumption, initial-run scheduling), append-only run bookkeeping (state-change detection, alert policy re-evaluation trigger), alert policy shape (signal source existence, threshold sanity), alert lifecycle (grouping, provider fan-out trigger, incident auto-resolve on last-alert close), incident lifecycle (P1 postmortem trigger, escalation scheduling), and provider config validation (per-provider JSON Schema, entitlement, credential encryption, test-mode dispatch).

handles: creating → populate ULID prefix `hck_`
creating → refuse when tenant does NOT hold `monitoring_capture` entitlement (returns `MONITORING_FEATURE_NOT_ENTITLED`)
creating → refuse when tenant does NOT hold `monitoring_health_check_slot` capacity (Small=5, Medium=25, Enterprise=∞)
creating → refuse when check_type = 'custom_script' AND tenant does NOT hold `monitoring_custom_metrics` entitlement (returns `MONITORING_CUSTOM_METRICS_NOT_ENTITLED`)
creating → validate check_type IS in the HealthCheckType enum (returns `MONITORING_INVALID_CHECK_TYPE`)
creating → validate target shape against the per-check-type target schema in data/check-type-catalog.json (returns `MONITORING_INVALID_CHECK_TARGET`)
creating → refuse when interval_seconds < 15 (rate-limit floor; returns `MONITORING_INTERVAL_TOO_SHORT`)
creating → refuse when timeout_seconds > interval_seconds (a timeout longer than the interval would overlap runs; returns `MONITORING_INVALID_TIMEOUT`)
creating → refuse when a non-deleted row already exists with (tenant_id, slug) — composite unique partial WHERE deleted_at IS NULL
creating → encrypt target at rest (contains connection strings + URLs + potentially credentials)
creating → set consecutive_failure_count = 0 + last_run_at = NULL + last_run_status = 'unknown'
creating → fire HealthCheckCreated
creating → dispatch RunHealthCheckJob delayed by interval_seconds/2 (avoids boot-time burst when many checks share the same interval)
updating (is_enabled toggled) → fire HealthCheckEnabled OR HealthCheckDisabled
updating (is_enabled → true) → dispatch RunHealthCheckJob
updating (is_enabled → false) → cancel pending RunHealthCheckJob via queue introspection
updating (interval_seconds changed) → validate new interval >= 15 seconds; refuse otherwise
updating (interval_seconds OR target changed) → re-schedule the next run at now() + new interval
updating (target changed) → re-encrypt at rest + re-validate against per-check-type schema
updating (check_type changed) → REFUSE — check_type is immutable after create (delete + recreate)
deleting (soft-delete) → cancel pending RunHealthCheckJob; releases 1 monitoring_health_check_slot unit
deleting (hard-delete) → allow only when soft-deleted > 90 days ago (grace period for audit)
creating → populate ULID prefix `hcr_`
creating → refuse when parent health_check is deleted (soft or hard); the run should not have been dispatched
creating → refuse when parent is_enabled = false (defense-in-depth against orphan runs from cancelled but not-yet-cancelled jobs)
creating → scan response_body jsonb for PII regex patterns (email, phone E.164, SSN, credit card) + redact with '[REDACTED]' placeholder
creating → truncate response_body to 8192 bytes (compact JSON serialization) before persist
creating → truncate error_details.error_message to 2048 chars
creating → fire HealthCheckRun event
creating → dispatch UpdateHealthCheckDenormalizedStateJob (updates parent health_check's last_run_at + last_run_status + consecutive_failure_count)
creating → when new run.status differs from parent.last_run_status: fire HealthCheckStatusChanged (which triggers policy re-evaluation)

creating → when new run.status = 'unhealthy' AND parent.consecutive_failure_count >= 7 (post-increment): fire MonitoringHealthCheckPersistentlyFailingNotification (leading indicator — this check has been failing for a while but hasn't yet crossed an alert threshold)
creating → when new run.status differs from parent.last_run_status: dispatch EvaluateAlertPoliciesJob(scoped_to_health_check_id=this_check)
updating (any field) → REFUSE ALL — runs are append-only per financial-audit shape
deleting → REFUSE except via PurgeExpiredHealthCheckRunsJob retention path
creating → populate ULID prefix `map_`
creating → refuse when tenant does NOT hold `monitoring_capture` entitlement
creating → refuse when tenant does NOT hold `monitoring_alert_policy_slot` capacity (Small=10, Medium=50, Enterprise=∞)
creating → refuse when signal_type IS not in the MonitoringSignalType enum (returns `MONITORING_INVALID_SIGNAL_TYPE`)
creating → refuse when signal_type = 'custom_metric' AND tenant does NOT hold `monitoring_custom_metrics` entitlement

creating → validate signal_source references a real subject: for signal_type = 'health_check_status', signal_source MUST be an existing health_check.slug in the same tenant; for signal_type IN ('error_rate', 'latency_p95', 'latency_p99', 'queue_depth', 'worker_health'), signal_source MUST match one of the emitted metric names; for signal_type = 'custom_metric', signal_source must be a registered custom metric name (returns `MONITORING_INVALID_SIGNAL_SOURCE`)
creating → validate severity IN {p1, p2, p3, p4} (returns `MONITORING_INVALID_SEVERITY`)
creating → validate threshold_operator IN {greater_than, less_than, equals, not_equals} (returns `MONITORING_INVALID_THRESHOLD_OPERATOR`)
creating → validate threshold_unit compatibility with signal_type (e.g. error_rate → 'percent'; latency_* → 'ms'; queue_depth → 'count')
creating → validate consecutive_breaches_required >= 1 (0 would fire on every eval tick — noise)
creating → validate window_seconds >= 60 (below 60s the evaluator has too few data points)
creating → validate notify_provider_configs is a non-empty array of active MonitoringProviderConfig ULIDs in the same tenant (returns `MONITORING_INVALID_NOTIFY_PROVIDERS`)
creating → validate suppression_config shape: { after_hours_suppress: bool, weekend_suppress: bool, silenced_until: ISO 8601 | null }
creating → fire MonitoringAlertPolicyCreated
updating (any threshold field OR signal fields changed) → invalidate the AlertPolicyEvaluator cache for this tenant
updating (suppression_config.silenced_until set OR after_hours_suppress / weekend_suppress toggled) → fire MonitoringAlertPolicySuppressed
updating (severity changed) → REFUSE when there are firing alerts against this policy (would confuse the incident-grouper); require all firing alerts resolved first
deleting (soft-delete) → refuse when firing alerts exist against this policy (returns `MONITORING_POLICY_HAS_ACTIVE_ALERTS`); releases 1 monitoring_alert_policy_slot unit
deleting (hard-delete) → allow only when soft-deleted > 90 days ago
creating → populate ULID prefix `mal_`
creating → refuse when parent monitoring_alert_policy is soft-deleted or disabled
creating → refuse when parent policy's suppression_config indicates a match (defense-in-depth against evaluator race; insert with status='suppressed' instead)

creating → automatically group into incident via the GroupAlertsIntoIncidentJob path: query for an existing firing incident with same severity + related signal_source within 5-min window; if found: set monitoring_incident_id; if not: leave NULL (GroupAlertsIntoIncidentJob will create the incident)
creating → set severity from parent policy.severity
creating → set status='firing' + fired_at = now()
creating → fire MonitoringAlertFired (triggers NotifyProvidersJob + GroupAlertsIntoIncidentJob)
creating (status='suppressed') → fire MonitoringAlertSuppressed instead of MonitoringAlertFired
updating (status → acknowledged) → require acknowledged_by_user_id present + tenant matches; set acknowledged_at
updating (status → acknowledged) → fire MonitoringAlertAcknowledged
updating (status → acknowledged) → dispatch MaybeAckIncidentJob (transitions parent incident to acknowledged if this is the last unacknowledged firing alert)
updating (status → resolved) → set resolved_at + resolved_by ('auto' | 'manual')
updating (status → resolved) → require resolved_by_user_id when resolved_by = 'manual'
updating (status → resolved) → fire MonitoringAlertResolved
updating (status → resolved) → dispatch MaybeResolveIncidentJob (transitions parent incident to resolved if this was the last firing alert)
updating (status → expired) → for firing alerts that stayed firing past policy expiration window; fire MonitoringAlertResolved with resolved_by='auto'
updating (severity OR signal_type OR signal_source changed) → REFUSE — alerts are per-fire immutable
deleting → REFUSE except via PurgeResolvedIncidentsJob retention path
creating → populate ULID prefix `mci_`
creating → set status='open' + opened_at = now() + alert_count = 1 (from the initial alert)
creating → default title from initial alert's signal_type + signal_source when not provided
creating → validate oncall_page_config shape: { primary_provider: <config_id>, escalation_policy: { after_seconds, escalation_provider: <config_id> } }
creating → fire MonitoringIncidentOpened (triggers NotifyProvidersJob for oncall_page_config)
creating → dispatch EscalateIncidentJob delayed by the severity's SLA (P1: 5min, P2: 15min, P3: 60min, P4: never)
updating (status → acknowledged) → require acknowledged_by_user_id present + tenant matches
updating (status → acknowledged) → cancel pending EscalateIncidentJob
updating (status → acknowledged) → fire MonitoringIncidentAcknowledged
updating (status → investigating | mitigating) → fire MonitoringIncidentStatusUpdated
updating (status → resolved) → set resolved_at + resolved_by
updating (status → resolved) → fire MonitoringIncidentResolved
updating (status → resolved) AND severity = 'p1' → dispatch MonitoringPostmortemRequiredNotification (schedules the postmortem doc) + transition status to 'postmortem' when postmortem doc is created
updating (severity changed) → REFUSE — incident severity is set at open + immutable
updating (alert_count changed) → allow only via observer's increment from GroupAlertsIntoIncidentJob (defense-in-depth against manual mutation)
deleting (soft-delete) → refuse when status != 'resolved' (must resolve first; returns `MONITORING_INCIDENT_NOT_RESOLVED`)
deleting (hard-delete) → allow only when soft-deleted > 90 days ago AND (severity != 'p1' OR tenant does NOT hold monitoring_extended_retention)
creating → populate ULID prefix `mpr_`
creating → refuse when tenant does NOT hold `monitoring_capture` entitlement
creating → refuse when tenant does NOT hold `monitoring_provider_slot` capacity (Small=1, Medium=3, Enterprise=∞)
creating → refuse when provider IN ('pagerduty', 'opsgenie') AND tenant does NOT hold `monitoring_paging` entitlement (returns `MONITORING_PAGING_NOT_ENTITLED`)
creating → refuse when provider = 'datadog' AND tenant does NOT hold `monitoring_apm` entitlement (returns `MONITORING_APM_NOT_ENTITLED`)
creating → refuse when provider = 'prometheus' AND tenant does NOT hold `monitoring_prometheus` entitlement (returns `MONITORING_PROMETHEUS_NOT_ENTITLED`)
creating → refuse when a non-deleted row already exists for (tenant_id, provider, name) — composite unique partial WHERE deleted_at IS NULL

creating → validate config jsonb against schemas/providers/<provider>-config.schema.json (per-provider — Sentry needs sentry_dsn; Datadog needs api_key + app_key; PagerDuty needs routing_key; Opsgenie needs api_key + team_id; Prometheus needs pushgateway_url; Custom Webhook needs webhook_url + hmac_secret) → refuse with `MONITORING_PROVIDER_CONFIG_INVALID`
creating → validate enabled_severities IS a non-empty subset of {p1, p2, p3, p4}
creating → encrypt config jsonb at rest (contains DSNs, API keys, HMAC secrets)
creating → default circuit_breaker_config from data/circuit-breaker-defaults.json when not provided
creating → default retry_config from data/retry-schedule-defaults.json when not provided
creating → set consecutive_failure_count = 0 + circuit_breaker_state = 'closed' + circuit_breaker_open_until = NULL
creating → fire MonitoringProviderConfigured
creating → when test_mode = true: dispatch MonitoringProviderTestJob (sends a synthetic P4 test alert via the provider's sandbox / test endpoint) → fires MonitoringProviderTestSent
updating (config jsonb changed) → re-validate against the per-provider JSON schema; refuse when invalid
updating (config jsonb changed) → re-encrypt at rest
updating (config jsonb changed) → fire MonitoringConfigurationChangedNotification (audit trail — the tenant admin needs to know credentials rotated)
updating (enabled_severities changed) → validate non-empty subset
updating (is_active flipped false) → fire MonitoringProviderDeactivated (reason='manual')
updating (circuit_breaker_state changed) → fire MonitoringProviderCircuitBreakerOpened OR MonitoringProviderCircuitBreakerClosed
updating (circuit_breaker_state → closed via manual reset) → increment circuit_breaker_reset_count
deleting (soft-delete) → refuse when circuit_breaker_state = 'open' (returns `MONITORING_PROVIDER_CIRCUIT_OPEN_ON_DEACTIVATE`; reset first)
deleting (soft-delete) → releases 1 monitoring_provider_slot unit; fires MonitoringProviderDeactivated (reason='manual')
deleting (hard-delete) → allow only when soft-deleted > 90 days ago
